item.title: Comprehensive Profiling of HIV Antibody Evolution
Developing Cross-Sectional Methods to Estimate HIV Incidence
item.description: HIV incidence can be determined by following cohorts of HIV-uninfected individuals and quantifying the number of new HIV cases or by using cross-sectional methods. Current cross-sectional methods of estimating HIV incidence utilize multi-assay algorithms (MAAs) to classify individuals as recently or non-recently infected. However, serologic assays that measure general antibody responses to HIV infection may be impacted by viral suppresion (innate or via antiretroviral therapy treatment (ART)), loss of CD4 T-cells, and other factors, resulting in inaccurate incidence estimates. Consequently, we are interested in developing more accurate methods for cross-sectional incidence estimation.
